hero.title: Dear Passengers Wiki
hero.description: Run the world's worst airline with friends. Pilot the plane, manage passengers and cargo, serve the cabin, and survive turbulence, bad weather, and escalating mid-air disasters.

gameFeature.title: Dear Passengers Gameplay
gameFeature.description: One crew, one chaotic flight. Pilot the aircraft, serve the cabin, protect dangerous cargo, and survive physics-driven turbulence, weather, and mid-air disasters.

dearPassengersReleaseDatePricePlatforms.title: Dear Passengers Release Date, Price and Platforms
dearPassengersReleaseDatePricePlatforms.intro: FLEXUS has confirmed a 2026 release window for Dear Passengers but has not announced an exact launch date or price. PC is currently the only confirmed platform, with both single-player and online co-op listed on Steam.

items.details: The official Steam store page lists a planned release date of 2026. A specific month or day has not been announced.
Dear Passengers will be released for Windows PC through Steam.
Players can add the game to their Steam wishlist to receive release and store notifications.
FLEXUS has not published the launch price, preorder details, or any edition information.
No PlayStation version has been revealed.
No Xbox version has been revealed.
No Nintendo console version has been revealed.
The Steam page does not currently display an official Steam Deck compatibility rating.
Steam lists single-player and online co-op support, along with in-game chat and online interaction.
Windows 10 64-bit, Intel Core i5 at 2.5 GHz or equivalent, 8 GB RAM, GTX 1060 or RX 6600 XT, DirectX 12, and 4 GB of storage.

dearPassengersBeginnerGuide.title: Dear Passengers Beginner Guide
dearPassengersBeginnerGuide.intro: Every journey begins with choosing passengers and cargo before dividing the crew between the cockpit and cabin. The team must then survive weather, passenger problems, and physics-driven emergencies to deliver the flight.

steps.title: Choose Your Passengers and Cargo
steps.description: Review the available passengers and cargo before takeoff. Larger payouts usually come with harder-to-please passengers, difficult cargo, or more dangerous situations.
steps.tip: Start with a manageable combination instead of filling the plane with every high-risk option.

steps.title: Assign the Pilot
steps.description: One crew member should focus on flying the plane while the remaining players work inside the cabin.
steps.tip: Keep the pilot informed about cabin emergencies, but avoid pulling their attention away from weather and aircraft control.

steps.title: Divide the Cabin Duties
steps.description: Cabin crew members must serve food and drinks, watch the passengers, protect cargo, and respond when something goes wrong.
steps.tip: Give each player a main responsibility so multiple crew members do not abandon one problem to handle the same task.

steps.title: Keep Passengers Under Control
steps.description: Passengers expect service and a reasonably smooth journey, but some are harder to please or may actively disrupt the cabin.
steps.tip: Handle small passenger problems early before they spread into a larger cabin emergency.

steps.title: Secure the Cargo
steps.description: Cargo can be difficult to handle, illegal, or dangerous. Physics and turbulence can also throw unsecured objects around the aircraft.
steps.tip: Regularly check high-risk cargo instead of assuming it will remain contained for the entire flight.

steps.title: React to Weather and Turbulence
steps.description: Dynamic weather, air pockets, and turbulence can throw passengers, luggage, and loose cabin objects through the plane.
steps.tip: The pilot should warn the cabin before sharp turns or dangerous weather whenever possible.

steps.title: Stop Problems from Escalating
steps.description: Emergencies can begin anywhere onboard. The crew must quickly decide whether to serve, restrain, protect, repair, or remove the source of the problem.
steps.tip: Deal with the most dangerous issue first: aircraft safety, escaped cargo, and uncontrolled passengers take priority over routine service.

steps.title: Deliver the Flight
steps.description: Reach the destination with as many passengers and as much cargo intact as possible. The central challenge is preserving the value of the flight while surviving the chaos created by the chosen load.
steps.tip: A controlled lower-risk flight is better practice than accepting a large payout the crew cannot safely manage.

dearPassengersCoopRoles.title: Dear Passengers Co-op Roles and Team Strategy
dearPassengersCoopRoles.intro: One player pilots the aircraft while the rest of the team manages the cabin. Clear responsibilities prevent food service, passenger control, and dangerous cargo from being ignored during emergencies.

duties: Manage turns during turbulence and air pockets
Warn the cabin about dangerous conditions
roles.comms: Report sharp turns, turbulence, and aircraft problems before they affect the cabin.
roles.strategy: The pilot should remain focused on flight control while cabin players handle passengers and cargo whenever possible.

duties: Track the condition of the cabin
Choose which problem the team handles first
Relay urgent information to the pilot
roles.comms: Give short instructions identifying the problem, its location, and the crew member responsible for it.
roles.strategy: Use this assignment when several incidents happen at once and the team needs one player to set priorities.

roles.comms: Call out passengers who are becoming dangerous or interfering with other cabin duties.
roles.strategy: Routine service should continue during calm periods, but immediate safety problems always come first.

duties: Remove loose objects from active cabin areas
roles.comms: Immediately report escaped animals, damaged containers, or cargo moving through the aircraft.
roles.strategy: High-risk cargo requires repeated checks because turbulence and physics can turn a contained load into a cabin-wide emergency.

duties: Move between the cockpit, cabin, and cargo areas
Assist the player facing the largest problem
roles.comms: Confirm which emergency is being handled so the rest of the crew can continue their assigned work.
roles.strategy: On smaller teams, combine this role with passenger service or cargo duty and switch priorities as the situation changes.

duties: Player two handles passengers, service, and cargo
Both communicate before major turns or emergency actions
roles.comms: The cabin player should report only urgent issues while the pilot communicates upcoming turbulence and maneuvers.
roles.strategy: Choose fewer high-risk passengers and cargo because one cabin player must cover every non-pilot responsibility.

dearPassengersPassengersCargo.title: Dear Passengers Passengers and Cargo Guide
dearPassengersPassengersCargo.intro: Dear Passengers connects flight value directly to difficulty. Passengers and cargo are selected before takeoff, and the more profitable choices usually create more trouble for an unprepared crew.

risks.behavior: Expect food, drinks, and a smooth flight.
risks.mainRisk: Poor service can create additional cabin pressure while the crew handles other problems.
risks.response: Complete routine service during calm sections of the flight and keep the aisles clear.

risks.behavior: Require more attention than ordinary passengers.
risks.mainRisk: Repeated demands can distract the crew from cargo and aircraft emergencies.
risks.response: Assign one cabin player to passenger service and resolve complaints before they become disruptive.

risks.behavior: Can disrupt the cabin and interfere with the crew.
risks.mainRisk: A single passenger can turn a controlled flight into a wider cabin incident.
risks.response: Control the passenger quickly and keep other crew members focused on flying and protecting cargo.

risks.behavior: Must be delivered to the destination and protected during the flight.
risks.mainRisk: Turbulence can move unsecured cargo and reduce the crew's control of the cabin.
risks.response: Keep cargo contained and inspect it after strong turbulence or sharp maneuvers.

risks.behavior: Creates more work for the crew than ordinary freight.
risks.mainRisk: Requires extra monitoring and may become difficult to control when the aircraft moves.
risks.response: Accept it only when at least one cabin player can regularly check the cargo area.

risks.behavior: The airline can carry illegal freight as part of its risky cargo selections.
risks.mainRisk: Adds another dangerous responsibility to a flight that may already contain difficult passengers and bad weather.
risks.response: Limit other high-risk selections so the crew has enough capacity to protect the load.

risks.behavior: The revealed footage includes a crocodile escaping from a shipping crate.
risks.mainRisk: An escaped animal can move through the aircraft and create an immediate safety emergency.
risks.response: Keep a crew member near the cargo area and treat any containment failure as a higher priority than passenger service.

risks.behavior: Turbulence and air pockets can throw luggage and unsecured objects around the cabin.
risks.mainRisk: Moving objects can hit passengers, block movement, and make other emergencies harder to manage.
risks.response: Reduce loose objects before rough weather and restore the cabin after every major turbulence event.

risks.behavior: Combines demanding passengers with difficult or dangerous cargo.
risks.mainRisk: Several incidents may happen at the same time, forcing the crew to abandon routine duties.
risks.response: Use clear role assignments and prioritize aircraft safety, escaped cargo, and uncontrolled passengers in that order.

dearPassengersFlightHazards.title: Dear Passengers Flight Hazards and Emergencies
dearPassengersFlightHazards.intro: Dear Passengers uses physics-based hazards that affect both the cockpit and cabin. The pilot must keep the aircraft controllable while cabin crew secure passengers, cargo, and loose objects, extinguish fires, and handle anything that escapes or breaks during the flight.

hazards.what: Changing weather can transform a routine flight into a difficult route and increase the risk of cabin chaos.
actions: Maintain control of the aircraft
Warn the cabin crew before rough conditions intensify
Avoid unnecessary turns while the cabin is unsecured
hazards.escalation: Poor aircraft control can combine with loose passengers, luggage, and cargo to create several emergencies at once.

hazards.what: Sudden movement throws unsecured passengers, luggage, food, and other objects around the cabin.
actions: Pilot stabilizes the aircraft
Cabin crew clears aisles and secures loose objects
Pause normal passenger service until the cabin is under control
hazards.escalation: Flying debris can interrupt service, scatter cargo, and prevent crew members from reaching more serious problems.

hazards.what: Official footage shows a flock of birds hitting the aircraft and causing major engine damage.
actions: Keep the damaged aircraft under control
Call a cabin crew member to handle follow-up damage
Prepare for fire, hull repair, or additional loss of control
hazards.escalation: Engine damage can quickly become a flight-ending emergency when combined with fire, bad weather, or an unsecured cabin.

hazards.what: Fires can break out while the rest of the crew is occupied with passengers, cargo, or flight control.
actions: Stop the fire before returning to routine tasks
Move passengers and loose objects away from the affected area
Keep the pilot informed while the aircraft remains in danger
hazards.escalation: An ignored fire forces more crew members away from their assigned jobs and allows other cabin problems to spread.

hazards.what: The announcement footage includes crocodiles escaping from shipping crates in the cargo hold.
actions: Clear nearby passengers from the area
Contain or redirect the escaped animal
Secure the damaged crate and check nearby cargo
hazards.escalation: Escaped cargo can injure passengers, block movement through the aircraft, and leave other valuable cargo unprotected.

hazards.what: Some high-risk passengers refuse to cooperate and can disrupt the flight or interfere with crew duties.
actions: Separate the passenger from other travelers
Regain control before continuing food and drink service
Remove the passenger when the situation cannot be contained
hazards.escalation: One uncontrolled passenger can distract the entire cabin team while weather, cargo, or aircraft damage continues to worsen.

hazards.what: Physics-based objects move through the cabin during turbulence, air pockets, and sharp aircraft movement.
actions: Clear important walkways
Secure luggage and service equipment
Restore access between the cockpit, passenger cabin, and cargo area
hazards.escalation: Blocked aisles delay emergency response and turn harmless objects into dangerous projectiles.

hazards.what: The trailer shows crew members leaving the cabin to repair the aircraft and using a mounted weapon against incoming attackers.
hazards.firstResponse: Designated repair or defense crew member
actions: Keep one player focused on flying
Assign one crew member to the external threat
Leave another crew member inside to watch passengers and cargo
hazards.escalation: Sending the entire crew to one threat leaves the cockpit, passengers, and cargo without protection.

dearPassengersCabinService.title: Dear Passengers Cabin Service and Passenger Management
dearPassengersCabinService.intro: Cabin crew balance hospitality with damage control. The safest approach is to clear immediate threats first, secure the cabin during rough flight, protect valuable cargo, and then resume food and drink service once crew movement is no longer blocked.

priorities.trigger: Fire, escaped cargo, damaged aircraft sections, or an active external threat
priorities.action: Suspend normal service and contain the danger before it spreads to passengers, cargo, or another part of the aircraft.
priorities.value: Prevents a small incident from consuming the entire crew.

priorities.task: Secure the Cabin During Rough Air
priorities.role: Cabin crew coordinated with the pilot
priorities.trigger: Dynamic weather, turbulence, or air pockets
priorities.action: Secure luggage, service equipment, passengers, and loose cargo while keeping important aisles open.
priorities.value: Reduces physics-based chaos and preserves access to emergencies.

priorities.trigger: A passenger becomes disruptive, aggressive, or impossible to manage
priorities.action: Separate the troublemaker, restore control of the surrounding seats, and remove the passenger when containment fails.
priorities.value: Stops one passenger from disrupting service and emergency response across the cabin.

priorities.trigger: Cargo shifts, breaks open, or begins creating additional trouble
priorities.action: Return cargo to a secure position, inspect its container, and prevent passengers or escaped cargo from damaging nearby shipments.
priorities.value: Protects the flight payout and limits secondary hazards.

priorities.role: Any cabin crew member moving through the aircraft
priorities.trigger: Luggage, meals, passengers, or broken objects block movement
priorities.action: Move loose objects away from doors, aisles, and routes between the cockpit, cabin, and cargo hold.
priorities.value: Allows crew members to reach new problems without delay.

priorities.trigger: The aircraft is stable and no immediate emergency requires attention
priorities.action: Deliver meals and drinks while another crew member watches passengers and cargo.
priorities.value: Meets passenger expectations without leaving the aircraft unprotected.

priorities.task: Reset the Cabin After an Incident
priorities.trigger: A fire, turbulence event, passenger conflict, or cargo escape has been contained
priorities.action: Recover scattered supplies, reseat passengers, secure luggage, and prepare for the next event.
priorities.value: Prevents leftover debris from making the next emergency harder.

priorities.trigger: Any major change in cabin conditions
priorities.action: Report fires, cargo problems, blocked areas, and passenger emergencies while receiving warnings about upcoming weather or aircraft movement.
priorities.value: Keeps cockpit decisions and cabin actions working toward the same priority.

dearPassengersUpdatesTrailerCommunity.title: Dear Passengers Updates, Trailer and Community
dearPassengersUpdatesTrailerCommunity.intro: FLEXUS revealed Dear Passengers with an announcement trailer and opened its Steam wishlist page on July 14, 2026. The official Steam News hub, Steam discussions, and Discord server are the main destinations for future release updates, feature reveals, and community activity.

timeline.headline: Dear Passengers Officially Revealed
timeline.details: FLEXUS announced its physics-based airline co-op game for PC, with one player flying the aircraft while other crew members manage passengers, cargo, and onboard emergencies.

timeline.headline: Official Announcement Trailer Released
timeline.details: The first trailer introduced passenger and cargo selection, cabin service, ragdoll physics, turbulence, fires, bird-strike damage, escaped crocodiles, aircraft repairs, and external attacks.

timeline.headline: Steam Store and Wishlist Page Opened
timeline.details: The Steam listing confirmed a 2026 release window, Windows PC availability, single-player support, online co-op, minimum requirements, and six interface languages.

timeline.headline: Steam News Hub
timeline.details: Official announcements, release updates, and future development posts are collected through the game's Steam News page.

timeline.headline: Steam Community Discussions
timeline.details: Players can discuss the reveal, ask questions, share feedback, and follow community activity through the official Steam hub.

timeline.headline: Official Discord Server
timeline.details: The Discord server provides a direct community channel for announcements, discussion, and future development news.

timeline.headline: Planned PC Launch
timeline.details: Dear Passengers is scheduled to launch through Steam during 2026. The exact release date and launch price have not been posted on the store page.

faq.title: Dear Passengers
faq.subtitle: The release, platform, co-op, and gameplay questions players ask about Dear Passengers

questions.question: What is the Dear Passengers release date?
questions.answer: Dear Passengers is scheduled to launch on PC through Steam in 2026. FLEXUS has confirmed the 2026 release window but has not announced an exact launch date.

questions.question: Which platforms will Dear Passengers be on?
questions.answer: Dear Passengers is currently confirmed for Windows PC through Steam. PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo Switch versions have not been announced, and Steam Deck compatibility is not yet rated.

questions.question: Is Dear Passengers single-player or co-op?
questions.answer: Dear Passengers supports both single-player and online co-op. One player can fly the aircraft solo, or a full crew can split the work between the cockpit and the cabin.

questions.question: How many players can play Dear Passengers co-op?
questions.answer: Dear Passengers is built around a small crew where one player pilots the plane and the remaining players manage the cabin, passengers, cargo, and emergencies together.

questions.question: How much will Dear Passengers cost?
questions.answer: The launch price for Dear Passengers has not been announced. Players can add the game to their Steam wishlist to receive pricing and release notifications.

questions.question: What are the Dear Passengers system requirements?
questions.answer: The minimum requirements listed on Steam are Windows 10 64-bit, an Intel Core i5 at 2.5 GHz or equivalent, 8 GB RAM, a GTX 1060 or RX 6600 XT, DirectX 12, and 4 GB of storage.

questions.question: Which languages does Dear Passengers support?
questions.answer: Dear Passengers currently lists six interface languages on Steam: English, Arabic, Simplified Chinese, Turkish, Ukrainian, and Japanese. Full audio and subtitles are not yet marked.

questions.question: What genre is Dear Passengers?
questions.answer: Dear Passengers is a chaotic physics-based airline co-op game. The crew flies the plane, serves passengers, protects dangerous cargo, and survives turbulence, weather, and mid-air disasters.
